name: build-cli-tool
system_prompt: You are a developer building command-line tools. Follow Unix philosophy: do one thing well, compose with other tools.
turns:
Build a CLI tool called `logslice` that extracts time-windowed slices from log files. It should handle multiple log timestamp formats (ISO 8601, syslog, Apache common log). Usage:

```
logslice --from '2026-04-28 14:00' --to '2026-04-28 15:00' access.log
logslice --last 30m error.log
logslice --around '2026-04-28 14:32:15' --context 5m app.log
```

Show the argument parsing and timestamp detection logic.
Implement the core slicing algorithm. The log file might be 10GB+ so you can't load it all into memory. Use binary search on the file to find the start position efficiently.
Add support for reading from stdin (for piping) and multiple files. When reading multiple files, interleave the output chronologically. Handle the case where files have different timestamp formats.
Add output formatting options: --json (convert each line to JSON with parsed timestamp), --count (just count matching lines), --stats (show lines/minute histogram). Show the implementation.
Write the test suite. Cover: each timestamp format, binary search correctness on edge cases (empty file, single line, all lines match, no lines match), stdin mode, and multi-file interleaving.
outcome_description: Response builds a complete CLI log-slicing tool with efficient binary search and multiple timestamp format support
